name: Cueza Villages and Cisneros Loop
name_original: Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Cisneros — circular por Cervatos de la Cueza
description: This moderate 27.9-mile mountain bike route near Cisneros offers 746 feet of gain and takes about 3 hours 26 minutes to complete.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Campos traverse the flat plains and river valleys of the Palencia province in Castile and León, Spain. The landscape features agricultural fields, historic canals like the Canal de Castilla, and small towns. This region offers a network of routes suitable for various skill levels, characterized by gentle gradients and open vistas. Best mountain bike trails around Campos The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque de Valdellano — circular desde Palencia, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain around Palencia. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Palencia a Husillos — circular por Canal de Castilla, a moderate 27.4 miles (44.0 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, offering scenic views of the waterway and surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo de Monzón de Campos — circular desde Palencia por el Castillo de Sarmiento, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and past historic sites, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Campos is defined by the Canal de Castilla, open plains, and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides along the canal to more challenging routes with moderate elevation gains. The routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Campos?
A: There are nearly 100 mountain bike trails around Campos, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy rides to more challenging routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Campos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Campos primarily traverse the flat plains and river valleys of the Palencia province. You can expect agricultural fields, historic canals like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, and small towns. The routes are generally characterized by gentle gradients and open vistas, with some moderate elevation gains on longer trails.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Campos?
A: Yes, Campos offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157650420/vuelta-a-palencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta a Palencia&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 13.1 km loop with minimal elevation change, ideal for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Campos offers routes with longer distances and moderate elevation gains. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1156130085/canal-of-castilla-and-camino-de-santiago-circular-route-by-the-king-s-bridge-from-fromista" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal of Castilla and Camino de Santiago — circular route by The King's Bridge from Frómista&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 69.4 km trail, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170249039/canal-de-castilla-osorno-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla - Osorno Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 80 km long.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails follow the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of the waterway and its engineering marvels like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;. You can also pass by historic sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Campos?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Campos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157684271/parque-de-valdellano-circular-desde-palencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Valdellano — circular desde Palencia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318383729/palencia-a-husillos-circular-por-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia a Husillos — circular por Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, both offering moderate rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Campos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the well-maintained paths along the Canal de Castilla and the opportunity to explore historic towns and open landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Campos?
A: Given the region's gentle gradients and open plains, many of the easier routes are suitable for families. Trails along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318320000/canal-de-castilla-grijota-loop-from-palencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla – Grijota Loop from Palencia&lt;/a&gt;, offer relatively flat and scenic rides that can be enjoyed by various ages and skill levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Campos?
A: While many outdoor areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for each trail or natural park. Given the agricultural nature of some areas, keeping dogs on a leash is advisable to protect livestock and wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Campos?
A: The best time to go mountain biking in Campos is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold, though the relatively flat terrain means trails are generally accessible year-round, weather permitting.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass through or near historic towns?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to connect or pass through historic towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318362127/castillo-de-monzon-de-campos-circular-desde-palencia-por-el-castillo-de-sarmiento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Monzón de Campos — circular desde Palencia por el Castillo de Sarmiento&lt;/a&gt; trail leads through agricultural landscapes and past historic sites, including the castle itself.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that follow the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, some mountain bike routes in Campos incorporate sections of the famous Camino de Santiago. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363804604/french-way-villalcazar-de-sirga-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way: Villalcázar de Sirga Loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such example, allowing riders to experience a part of this historic pilgrimage route.
tips: This is really a small town with not many people but it has a nice church that is worth visiting. It is called Church of San Miguel.

Here, at the top of the hill, with giant windmills all around, you have a spectacular view of the countryside below. This is the highest point in the area so it is easy to see for miles and miles. There are some information signs that explain the area.
The population of this town has decreased from over 2,000 in the 19th century to only around 400 today, but it still has some lovely churches and buildings to stop and admire.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 45 km (28 miles) long and is estimated to take around 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route, typical of the Palencia region, is varied. You can expect a mix of compacted dirt tracks, natural trails, and potentially some low-traffic secondary roads. While the overall difficulty is moderate, some sections might involve features like berms or rock gardens, requiring good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' difficulty, this route is best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience and good physical fitness. While not extremely technical, it's not ideal for absolute beginners due to its length and varied terrain. Riders should be comfortable with different surfaces and some elevation changes.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the Church of San Miguel of Mazuecos de Valdeginate, and the Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint, which provides panoramic views. The route also takes you through Cisneros Village, offering a glimpse into local life and architecture.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit for mountain biking in this region is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially challenging trail conditions.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route are not available, many trails in the Palencia region are dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and to carry water for them. Please ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking on public trails in this area of Palencia does not require a specific permit or entrance fee. However, always respect private property and local regulations, especially if the route passes through agricultural land or protected areas.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking for the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting near Cervatos de la Cueza, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Cervatos de la Cueza village. Look for designated public parking areas or suitable roadside spots that do not obstruct local traffic or access.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas), Camino de Cervatos de la Cueza a Riberos de la Cueza, and Camino Villanueva del Rebollar - Villamuera de la Cueza.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: The villages along or near the route, such as Cisneros and Cervatos de la Cueza, may offer limited options for cafes, restaurants, or rural accommodation. It's advisable to check in advance for availability, especially during off-peak seasons, or to carry sufficient provisions for your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and a first-aid kit. A GPS device or a downloaded map of the route is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel of Mazuecos de Valdeginate
